explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 80 from a base nutrition score of 77. Context lifted it by 3 points.

topPros.title: extreme fibre density
topPros.explanation: Adds standout digestive and satiety value even beyond the headline nutrient display.

topCons.title: contains some anti-nutrients
topCons.explanation: Phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

topCons.title: texture can put some people off
topCons.explanation: The gel-like texture is not universally appealing.

topCons.title: usually works best with some preparation
topCons.explanation: Often needs soaking or mixing into something else to shine.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 3 points.

closing.summary: Chia Seeds stands out for extreme fibre density and can be very filling for the amount used. but it also gets held back by contains some anti-nutrients and texture can put some people off. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.useCaseNote: Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
